stories.title: Side-by-side HTML diff report: ks diff-report
stories.description:
## Parent PRD

#1

## What to build

An HTML report that presents snapshot diffs visually with side-by-side comparison. This slice introduces:

- `ks diff-report [snapshot-id] [--output path]` — generates an HTML report comparing a snapshot (default: latest) against the baseline.

The report layout:
- **For each URL path and each breakpoint**: baseline screenshot on the left, current screenshot on the right, pixel diff overlay in the center (from #5).
- **Below each URL section**: audit delta tables showing category, before count, after count, and delta. Element change lists showing selector, change type (appeared/disappeared/moved/resized), and details.
- Screenshots are base64-embedded (matching the existing `ks report` approach).

Extends the existing `report/` HTML template engine. The report is self-contained — a single HTML file that can be opened in any browser.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `ks diff-report` generates a self-contained HTML file comparing latest snapshot vs baseline
- [ ] Side-by-side layout shows baseline (left), current (right), and diff overlay (center) for each URL and breakpoint
- [ ] Audit delta tables show per-category before/after/delta counts
- [ ] Element change lists show each change with selector, type, and details
- [ ] `--output` flag controls output path; defaults to `.kaleidoscope/diff-report.html`
- [ ] Screenshots are base64-embedded in the HTML
- [ ] Output follows `output.Result` JSON convention (returns path to generated report)

## Blocked by

- Blocked by #4 (audit/element diff engine)
- Blocked by #5 (screenshot pixel diff)

## User stories addressed

- User story 3: side-by-side HTML report with highlighted differences
- User story 13: audit delta tables in the diff report
acceptanceCriteria: Loop dispatch metadata includes repository and issue number.
`ks diff-report [snapshot-id] [--output path]` — generates an HTML report comparing a snapshot (default: latest) against the baseline.
**For each URL path and each breakpoint**: baseline screenshot on the left, current screenshot on the right, pixel diff overlay in the center (from #5).

**Below each URL section**: audit delta tables showing category, before count, after count, and delta. Element change lists showing selector, change type (appeared/disappeared/moved/resized), and details.
Negative case: invalid inputs are rejected with a clear error.
